def solve(nums, p, queries)
  n = nums.length
  
  # GCD function
  def gcd(a, b)
    while b != 0
      a, b = b, a % b
    end
    a
  end

  # Check if a good subsequence exists
  def check_good_subsequence(current_nums, p)
    n = current_nums.length
    
    # A good subsequence must have length < n and GCD == p.
    # We need to check for any non-empty subsequence S where:
    # 1. |S| < n
    # 2. gcd(elements in S) == p

    # Case 1: Subsequences of length 1
    # If any element nums[i] == p, then {nums[i]} is a subsequence of length 1 < n (since n >= 2),
    # and gcd({nums[i]}) = p.
    return true if current_nums.any? { |x| x == p }

    # Case 2: Subsequences of length >= 2
    # The GCD of a set of numbers is p if and only if every number in the set is a multiple of p,
    # AND the GCD of the numbers divided by p is 1.
    
    # Filter elements that are multiples of p
    multiples_of_p = current_nums.select { |x| x % p == 0 }
    
    # If there are less than 2 multiples of p, we can't form a subsequence of length >= 2.
    # We already checked length 1 in Case 1.
    return false if multiples_of_p.length < 2
    
    # Check if the GCD of all pairs (or all) of multiples of p, after dividing by p, is 1.
    # Since we are looking for *existence*, we only need to find *one* subsequence S of length >= 2
    # such that gcd(S) = p.
    
    # Let's check if there exist two multiples, a and b, such that gcd(a/p, b/p) = 1.
    # If such a pair exists, then gcd(a, b) = p * gcd(a/p, b/p) = p * 1 = p.
    # Since n >= 2, a subsequence of length 2 exists and is < n.
    
    # We need to check if there is *any* subset of multiples_of_p of size >= 2 whose GCD is p.
    # A necessary and sufficient condition for the existence of such a subsequence is:
    # The GCD of ALL multiples_of_p must be p, AND the size of multiples_of_p must be >= 2.
    # Wait, this is not sufficient. Example: [6, 12, 18], p=2. Multiples are all of them. GCD is 6.
    # Subsequence [6, 12] -> GCD 6. Subsequence [6, 18] -> GCD 6. Subsequence [12, 18] -> GCD 6.
    # No subsequence has GCD 2.
    
    # The correct condition is:
    # Does there exist a subset S of multiples_of_p, |S| >= 2, such that gcd(elements in S) = p?

    # If we find two elements a, b in multiples_of_p such that gcd(a/p, b/p) = 1, we are done.
    # If all pairs have gcd > 1 (after dividing by p), then the GCD of any larger set containing them will also be > 1 (after dividing by p).
    
    # Let's test all pairs of distinct multiples of p.
    m_div_p = multiples_of_p.map { |x| x / p }
    
    m_div_p.each_with_index do |m1, i|
      (i + 1).upto(m_div_p.length - 1) do |j|
        m2 = m_div_p[j]
        if gcd(m1, m2) == 1
          # Found a pair (a, b) such that gcd(a/p, b/p) = 1, so gcd(a, b) = p.
          return true
        end
      end
    end
    
    # What if the only good subsequence has length >= 3? e.g., [6, 10, 15], p=1.
    # Here, m_div_p = [6, 10, 15].
    # gcd(6, 10)=2. gcd(6, 15)=3. gcd(10, 15)=5. No pair works.
    # But gcd(6, 10, 15) = 1. So a length 3 subsequence exists.
    
    # If no pair works, it means for all pairs (a', b'), gcd(a', b') = g' > 1.
    # This implies that all elements in m_div_p share a common factor g' > 1.
    # If all elements share a common factor g' > 1, then the GCD of the entire set m_div_p is at least g'.
    # Therefore, the GCD of any subset of m_div_p is at least g'.
    # Thus, gcd(S) >= g' > 1 for any subset S of size >= 2.
    
    # So, checking pairs is sufficient.
    return false
  end

  count = 0
  current_nums = nums.dup

  queries.each do |query|
    ind, val = query
    
    # 1. Update the array
    current_nums[ind] = val
    
    # 2. Check for existence of a good subsequence
    if check_good_subsequence(current_nums, p)
      count += 1
    end
  end

  count
end
